DISCOVERY 302, RULING - A R G U E D. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ANNA LUI, LILY LUI, RAYMOND LUI, NG LUI'S MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFFS SUBPOENAS OF TAX RECORDS AND ACCOUNTANT COMMUNICATIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFFS' SUBPOENA IS GRANTED AS TO THOSE CATEGORIES AND TESTIMONY RELATING TO PROPERTY AND FINANCIAL INFORMATION UNRELATED TO THE 1675 CLAY STREET PROPERTY, SPECIFICALLY, DOCUMENT REQUESTS 11, 13 - 16. PLAINTIFFS' HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN TO REQUIRE PRODUCTION OF BROAD PERSONAL FINANCIAL RECORDS UNRELATED TO THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. THE WITNESS SHALL NOT BE REQUIRED TO PROVIDE TESTIMONY AS TO THE CATEGORIES OF DOCUMENTS SET FORTH ABOVE. PLAINTIFF SHALL BE ALLOWED TO QUESTION DEPONENT REGARDING THE DISPOSITION OF REVENUE FROM THE 1675 CLAY STREET PROPERTY TO OTHER PROPERTIES, BUT SHALL NOT BE REQUIRED TO RESPOND TO QUESTIONS REGARDING THE ENTITIES/INDIVIDUALS TO WHOM THOSE REVENUES WERE DIRECTED. THE WITNESS SHALL PRODUCE DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DOCUMENT REQUEST 1 - 10, REQUEST 12, LIMITED TO THE 1675 CLAY STREET PROPERTY, REQUESTS 17 - 18, AND REQUESTS 19 - 24 LIMITED TO THE 1675 CLAY STREET PROPERTY, INCLUDING ALL DOCUMENTS RELATED TO SCHEDULE E FOR THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, AND THE WITNESS SHALL PROVIDE TESTIMONY REGARDING THE PERMITTED CATEGORIES. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM STEPHANIE KRMPOTIC AT JSKRMPOTICE@LOWBALL.COM. Notice Of Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoenas Of Tax Records And Accountant Communications And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mary M. Sun, Esq., 626-616-6526, appearing for Plaintiff; (CourtCall)
Nelson W. Goodell, Esq., 415-495-3950, appearing for Defendants Anna Yit Nor Lui et al

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANNA LUI, LILY LUI, RAYMOND LUI, NG LUI Notice Of Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoenas Of Tax Records And Accountant Communications And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendants' Motion to Quash Plaintiffs' subpoena is granted as to those categories and testimony relating to property and financial information unrelated to the 1675 Clay Street property, specifically, document requests 11, 13 - 16. Plaintiffs' have not met their burden to require production of broad personal financial records unrelated to the subject property. The witness shall not be required to provide testimony as to the categories of documents set forth above. Plaintiff shall be allowed to question deponent regarding the disposition of revenue from the 1675 Clay Street property to other properties, but shall not be required to respond to questions regarding the entities/individuals to whom those revenues were directed.

The witness shall produce documents responsive to document request 1 - 10, request 12, limited to the 1675 Clay Street property, requests 17 - 18, and requests 19 - 24 limited to the 1675 Clay Street property, including all documents related to schedule E for the subject property, and the witness shall provide testimony regarding the permitted categories.

Request for Sanctions are denied.

Prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312 by email to Judge Pro Tem Stephanie Krmpotic at jskrmpotice@lowball.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Defendant Anna Lui, Lily Lui, Raymond Lui, Ng Lui Notice Of Motion To Quash (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only, Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Feb-27-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 27, 2019, Line 4, Defendants Anna Lui, Lily Lui, Raymond Lui, Ng Lui Motion To Quash (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only, Part 2 Of 2)

This is not necessarily a reasonable assumption. First, it does not necessarily follow that because the Wells Fargo account was only used for the apartment's rents and expenses, every deposit into the account constituted "missing rental income." Standing alone, the single deposit on one occasion in 2017 from a Chase bank account does not necessarily lead to any particular conclusion about the Chase account. Here, Defendants have provided an explanation for the Chase deposit - that is, it was money that was being distributed to the family members from the Chase account when it was closed after the father's death, and Defendants have provided photocopies of distribution checks that corroborate this story. Plaintiffs have questioned this explanation. But even if the Chase explanation were incorrect, it does not necessarily follow that the Chase account was used as a repository for absconded rental funds. Plaintiffs already have obtained discovery of bank records of the four bank accounts that were actually used for the rental property; tax returns for the years in which the parties co-owned the property; and spreadsheets showing the amount of rent and expense from the rental business. If there was, as alleged, a breach of an oral agreement to share profits from the rental property business, that should be demonstrated by the documents that plaintiff has discovered. And even assuming that more discovery were warranted about the single payment from the Chase account in April 2017, it can be obtained through more narrowly tailored discovery than the broad subpoenas that were served on Chase. For the foregoing reasons, the information sought in the Chase subpoena is not "essential to determining the truth" of the matters in dispute in this case.

Plaintiffs also argue that the motion should be denied because it was not accompanied by a separate statement. But a separate statement is not required as part of a motion to quash a subpoena for the production of business records other than at a deposition. See CRC 3.1345(a). The subpoenas at issue on this motion do not seek the production of documents at a deposition.

Defendants request judicial notice of Plaintiffs' complaint and an earlier filed motion to quash in this matter. The request is granted only as to the fact of the filing of the two documents, but not as to any of the contents of the two documents.

Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of tentative ruling part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)
